sourceRef.citation: Kim Van De Wiel et al. 2027

statement.en: Disease ecology at a wildlife-livestock interface: Gastrointestinal parasite sharing in wild and domestic African ungulates

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom concerns empirical disease ecology research at wildlife-livestock interfaces. This is an active field with ongoing data collection, validation, and interpretation. The 2026 date indicates this is recent/provisional work not yet through full peer-review cycles. Gastrointestinal parasite sharing dynamics are context-dependent and subject to environmental, seasonal, and population variables, making absolute truth-claims premature.

ramanujanFormula.latex: \frac{\partial P}{\partial t} = \alpha W \cdot L + \beta(W \cap L) - \gamma P
ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 野生動物と家畜の接触が寄生虫の流行を増幅させる、見えない橋のようだ。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 二つの個体群が接する場所で、寄生虫は静寂のうちに交換される。
